95: // Runs the controller and returns an error explaining why running was stopped.
96: // Reconciliation ends as soon as the context completes. If there are events
97: // waiting to be processed at that itme, they will be dropped.
98: func (c *controller[T]) Run(ctx context.Context) error {
99: 	klog.Infof("starting %s", c.options.Name)
100: 	defer klog.Infof("stopping %s", c.options.Name)
101: 
102: 	c.queue = workqueue.NewTypedRateLimitingQueueWithConfig(
103: 		workqueue.DefaultTypedControllerRateLimiter[string](),
104: 		workqueue.TypedRateLimitingQueueConfig[string]{Name: c.options.Name},
105: 	)
106: 
107: 	// Forcefully shutdown workqueue. Drop any enqueued items.
108: 	// Important to do this in a `defer` at the start of `Run`.
109: 	// Otherwise, if there are any early returns without calling this, we
110: 	// would never shut down the workqueue
111: 	defer c.queue.ShutDown()
112: 
113: 	enqueue := func(obj interface{}, isInInitialList bool) {
114: 		var key string
115: 		var err error
116: 		if key, err = cache.DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc(obj); err != nil {
117: 			utilruntime.HandleError(err)
118: 			return
119: 		}
120: 		if isInInitialList {
121: 			c.hasProcessed.Start(key)
122: 		}
123: 
124: 		c.queue.Add(key)
125: 	}
126: 
127: 	registration, err := c.informer.AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerDetailedFuncs{
128: 		AddFunc: enqueue,
129: 		UpdateFunc: func(oldObj, newObj interface{}) {
130: 			oldMeta, err1 := meta.Accessor(oldObj)
131: 			newMeta, err2 := meta.Accessor(newObj)
132: 
133: 			if err1 != nil || err2 != nil {
134: 				if err1 != nil {
135: 					utilruntime.HandleError(err1)
136: 				}
137: 
138: 				if err2 != nil {
139: 					utilruntime.HandleError(err2)
140: 				}
141: 				return
142: 			} else if oldMeta.GetResourceVersion() == newMeta.GetResourceVersion() {
143: 				if len(oldMeta.GetResourceVersion()) == 0 {
144: 					klog.Warningf("%v throwing out update with empty RV. this is likely to happen if a test did not supply a resource version on an updated object", c.options.Name)
145: 				}
146: 				return
147: 			}
148: 
149: 			enqueue(newObj, false)
150: 		},
151: 		DeleteFunc: func(obj interface{}) {
152: 			// Enqueue
153: 			enqueue(obj, false)
154: 		},
155: 	})
156: 
157: 	// Error might be raised if informer was started and stopped already
158: 	if err != nil {
159: 		return err
160: 	}
161: 
162: 	c.notificationsDelivered.Store(registration.HasSynced)
163: 
164: 	// Make sure event handler is removed from informer in case return early from
165: 	// an error
166: 	defer func() {
167: 		c.notificationsDelivered.Store(func() bool { return false })
168: 		// Remove event handler and Handle Error here. Error should only be raised
169: 		// for improper usage of event handler API.
170: 		if err := c.informer.RemoveEventHandler(registration); err != nil {
171: 			utilruntime.HandleError(err)
172: 		}
173: 	}()
174: 
175: 	// Wait for initial cache list to complete before beginning to reconcile
176: 	// objects.
177: 	if !cache.WaitForNamedCacheSync(c.options.Name, ctx.Done(), c.informer.HasSynced) {
178: 		// ctx cancelled during cache sync. return early
179: 		err := ctx.Err()
180: 		if err == nil {
181: 			// if context wasnt cancelled then the sync failed for another reason
182: 			err = errors.New("cache sync failed")
183: 		}
184: 		return err
185: 	}
186: 
187: 	waitGroup := sync.WaitGroup{}
188: 
189: 	for i := uint(0); i < c.options.Workers; i++ {
190: 		waitGroup.Add(1)
191: 		go func() {
192: 			defer waitGroup.Done()
193: 			wait.Until(c.runWorker, time.Second, ctx.Done())
194: 		}()
195: 	}
196: 
197: 	klog.Infof("Started %v workers for %v", c.options.Workers, c.options.Name)
198: 
199: 	// Wait for context cancel.
200: 	<-ctx.Done()
201: 
202: 	// Forcefully shutdown workqueue. Drop any enqueued items.
203: 	c.queue.ShutDown()
204: 
205: 	// Workqueue shutdown signals for workers to stop. Wait for all workers to
206: 	// clean up
207: 	waitGroup.Wait()
208: 
209: 	// Only way for workers to ever stop is for caller to cancel the context
210: 	return ctx.Err()
211: }
212: 
213: func (c *controller[T]) HasSynced() bool {
214: 	return c.hasProcessed.HasSynced()
215: }
216: 
217: func (c *controller[T]) runWorker() {
218: 	for {
219: 		key, shutdown := c.queue.Get()
220: 		if shutdown {
221: 			return
222: 		}
223: 
224: 		// We wrap this block in a func so we can defer c.workqueue.Done.
225: 		err := func(obj string) error {
226: 			// We call Done here so the workqueue knows we have finished
227: 			// processing this item. We also must remember to call Forget if we
228: 			// do not want this work item being re-queued. For example, we do
229: 			// not call Forget if a transient error occurs, instead the item is
230: 			// put back on the workqueue and attempted again after a back-off
231: 			// period.
232: 			defer c.queue.Done(obj)
233: 			defer c.hasProcessed.Finished(key)
234: 
235: 			if err := c.reconcile(key); err != nil {
236: 				// Put the item back on the workqueue to handle any transient errors.
237: 				c.queue.AddRateLimited(key)
238: 				return fmt.Errorf("error syncing '%s': %s, requeuing", key, err.Error())
239: 			}
240: 			// Finally, if no error occurs we Forget this item so it is allowed
241: 			// to be re-enqueued without a long rate limit
242: 			c.queue.Forget(obj)
243: 			klog.V(4).Infof("syncAdmissionPolicy(%q)", key)
244: 			return nil
245: 		}(key)
246: 
247: 		if err != nil {
248: 			utilruntime.HandleError(err)
249: 		}
250: 	}
251: }
252: 
253: func (c *controller[T]) reconcile(key string) error {
254: 	var newObj T
255: 	var err error
256: 	var namespace string
257: 	var name string
258: 	var lister NamespacedLister[T]
259: 
260: 	// Convert the namespace/name string into a distinct namespace and name
261: 	namespace, name, err = cache.SplitMetaNamespaceKey(key)
262: 	if err != nil {
263: 		utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("invalid resource key: %s", key))
264: 		return nil
265: 	}
266: 
267: 	if len(namespace) > 0 {
268: 		lister = c.informer.Namespaced(namespace)
269: 	} else {
270: 		lister = c.informer
271: 	}
272: 
273: 	newObj, err = lister.Get(name)
274: 	if err != nil {
275: 		if !kerrors.IsNotFound(err) {
276: 			return err
277: 		}
278: 
279: 		// Deleted object. Inform reconciler with empty
280: 	}
281: 
282: 	return c.reconciler(namespace, name, newObj)
283: }

The key finding is: **kubernetes/kubernetes does NOT use sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime**. It has its own internal controller manager framework. The task is asking about the internal pattern that is analogous to controller-runtime.

Here's the complete picture:

## The Kubernetes Internal Controller Manager Framework

### 1. The "Reconciler" Interface Equivalent

The kubernetes codebase has two relevant interfaces:

**`controller.Interface`** (staging/src/k8s.io/controller-manager/controller/interfaces.go):
```go
type Interface interface {
    Name() string
}
```
This is the base interface for controllers. It's analogous to `reconcile.Reconciler` in controller-runtime.

**`InitFunc`** (cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go):
```go
type InitFunc func(ctx context.Context, controllerContext ControllerContext, controllerName string) (controller controller.Interface, enabled bool, err error)
```
This is the function type that initializes a controller. It's analogous to `SetupWithManager` in controller-runtime.

### 2. The "SetupWithManager" Equivalent

Each controller has a `newXxxControllerDescriptor()` function that creates a `ControllerDescriptor` with the controller's name and init function. This is analogous to `SetupWithManager` in controller-runtime.

For example, in `cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/core.go`:
```go
func newNodeIpamControllerDescriptor() *ControllerDescriptor {
    return &ControllerDescriptor{
        name:     names.NodeIpamController,
        aliases:  []string{"nodeipam"},
        initFunc: startNodeIpamController,
    }
}
```

### 3. The "Manager" Equivalent

The `ControllerContext` struct (cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go) is analogous to the controller-runtime `Manager`. It provides:
- `ClientBuilder` - for creating clients
- `InformerFactory` - for creating informers
- `RESTMapper` - for REST mapping
- `InformersStarted` - channel for informer sync

### 4. Registration with the Manager

`NewControllerDescriptors()` (cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go) is the registration function that collects all controller descriptors. This is analogous to the controller-runtime manager's `Add()` method.

### 5. The Reconcile Loop

`StartControllers()` and `StartController()` (cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go) start all controllers. Each controller's `initFunc` is called, which creates the controller and starts its reconcile loop (typically via `go controller.Run(ctx)`).

### 6. The Generic Controller Pattern (for admission policy controllers)

The `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/policy/internal/generic` package has a more controller-runtime-like pattern:
- `Controller[T]` interface with `Run()`, `Informer()`, `HasSynced()` methods
- `controller[T]` struct with a `reconciler` function
- The `reconcile()` method is called from the worker loop

Actually, I have enough information. Let me now write the answer.json file.

The key findings are:

1. **kubernetes/kubernetes does NOT use sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime**. The vendor directory does not contain controller-runtime. The only reference to "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime" is in a test comment.

2. **The kubernetes codebase has its own internal controller manager framework** that is analogous to controller-runtime. The key components are:

   a. **`controller.Interface`** - defined in `staging/src/k8s.io/controller-manager/controller/interfaces.go` - This is the base interface for controllers (analogous to `reconcile.Reconciler` in controller-runtime). It requires only a `Name() string` method.

   b. **`InitFunc`** - defined in `cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go` - This is the function type that initializes a controller. It's analogous to `SetupWithManager` in controller-runtime.

   c. **`ControllerDescriptor`** - defined in `cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go` - This wraps a controller's name and init function.

   d. **`ControllerContext`** - defined in `cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go` - This is the "Manager" equivalent, providing clients, informers, and other shared resources.

   e. **`NewControllerDescriptors()`** - defined in `cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go` - This registers all controllers.

   f. **`StartControllers()`** and **`StartController()`** - defined in `cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go` - These start all controllers.

3. **The registration pattern** is:
   - Each controller has a `newXxxControllerDescriptor()` function that creates a `ControllerDescriptor` with the controller's name and init function.
   - The `ControllerDescriptor` is registered in `NewControllerDescriptors()`.
   - When the controller manager starts, `StartControllers()` is called, which calls `StartController()` for each controller.
   - `StartController()` calls the controller's `initFunc`, which creates the controller and starts its reconcile loop (typically via `go controller.Run(ctx)`).

4. **The reconcile loop** is implemented by each controller individually, typically using a `workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface` and a `Run()` method that starts worker goroutines.

5. **The generic controller pattern** (for admission policy controllers) in `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/policy/internal/generic` has a more controller-runtime-like pattern with a `Controller[T]` interface and a `reconciler` function.

steps.message:
The `/workspace/answer.json` has been written. Here is a complete summary of all findings:

---

## How a Controller Registers with the "Manager" in kubernetes/kubernetes v1.32.0

### Critical Finding: No controller-runtime

`sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime` is **completely absent** from `kubernetes/kubernetes`. The vendor directory under `vendor/sigs.k8s.io/` contains only `json`, `yaml`, `kustomize`, `structured-merge-diff`, `knftables`, and `apiserver-network-proxy`. The only occurrence of the string `"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime"` in the entire repo is a comment in a test file. There is no `Reconciler` interface, no `SetupWithManager`, and no `ctrl.Manager` from that library.

Instead, the codebase uses its own internal controller manager framework (`k8s.io/controller-manager`). Here is the complete trace:

---

### 1. The "Reconciler Interface" Equivalent

**File:** `staging/src/k8s.io/controller-manager/controller/interfaces.go`

```go
type Interface interface {
    Name() string
}
```

This is the base interface every controller must satisfy. Two optional extensions exist:
- `controller.Debuggable` → `DebuggingHandler() http.Handler` (mounts `/debug/controllers/<name>/`)
- `controller.HealthCheckable` → `HealthChecker() healthz.UnnamedHealthChecker` (exposes `/healthz` check)

---

### 2. The "SetupWithManager" Equivalent — `InitFunc` + `ControllerDescriptor`

**File:** `cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go`

```go
// InitFunc is the "SetupWithManager" equivalent
type InitFunc func(ctx context.Context, controllerContext ControllerContext, controllerName string) (controller controller.Interface, enabled bool, err error)

// ControllerDescriptor binds a name to its InitFunc
type ControllerDescriptor struct {
    name                      string
    initFunc                  InitFunc
    requiredFeatureGates      []featuregate.Feature
    aliases                   []string
    isDisabledByDefault       bool
    isCloudProviderController bool
    requiresSpecialHandling   bool
}
```

Each controller defines a `newXxxControllerDescriptor()` factory and a `startXxxController()` InitFunc. Example from `cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/networking.go`:

```go
func newServiceCIDRsControllerDescriptor() *ControllerDescriptor {
    return &ControllerDescriptor{
        name:     names.ServiceCIDRController,
        initFunc: startServiceCIDRsController,
        requiredFeatureGates: []featuregate.Feature{features.MultiCIDRServiceAllocator},
    }
}

func startServiceCIDRsController(ctx context.Context, controllerContext ControllerContext, controllerName string) (controller.Interface, bool, error) {
    go servicecidrs.NewController(ctx, ...).Run(ctx, 5)
    return nil, true, nil
}
```

---

### 3. The "Manager" Equivalent — `ControllerContext`

**File:** `cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go` (lines 365–401)

`ControllerContext` is passed to every `InitFunc` and provides all shared resources:
- `ClientBuilder` — creates per-controller REST clients
- `InformerFactory` — `SharedInformerFactory` for typed informers
- `ObjectOrMetadataInformerFactory` — typed + metadata informers
- `RESTMapper` — deferred discovery REST mapper
- `InformersStarted` — channel closed after all controllers initialize
- `ResyncPeriod`, `ControllerManagerMetrics`, `GraphBuilder`

---

### 4. Registration — `NewControllerDescriptors()`

**File:** `cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go` (lines 492–593)

This is the central registration table. An inner `register()` closure validates and inserts each `ControllerDescriptor` into a `map[string]*ControllerDescriptor`. All ~40 built-in controllers are registered here:

```go
register(newEndpointsControllerDescriptor())
register(newDeploymentControllerDescriptor())
register(newServiceCIDRsControllerDescriptor())
register(newStorageVersionMigratorControllerDescriptor())
// ... ~40 total
```

---

### 5. Wiring into the Reconcile Loop

**File:** `cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go`

The call chain is:

```
Run(ctx, c)
  └─ run(ctx, controllerDescriptors)
       ├─ CreateControllerContext()   → builds the ControllerContext
       ├─ StartControllers()          → iterates all descriptors
       │    └─ StartController()      → for each descriptor:
       │         ├─ checks feature gates, enabled status
       │         ├─ calls initFunc(ctx, controllerCtx, name)
       │         │    └─ constructs controller, launches: go controller.Run(ctx)
       │         └─ optionally mounts debug handler + health checker
       └─ InformerFactory.Start()     → starts all shared informers
```

`StartController()` (lines 708–767) is the key function: it calls the `initFunc`, which constructs the controller and starts its own goroutine-based reconcile loop. Each controller's `Run()` method starts worker goroutines that pull keys from a `workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface` and call a `syncXxx()` / `reconcile()` method.

---

### 6. Generic Controller Pattern (Admission Policy Controllers)

A more controller-runtime-like pattern exists in `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/policy/internal/generic/`:

- **`interface.go`**: `Controller[T runtime.Object]` interface with `Run()`, `Informer()`, `HasSynced()`
- **`controller.go`**: `controller[T]` struct with a `reconciler func(namespace, name string, newObj T) error` callback; `Run()` registers informer event handlers → enqueues keys → worker goroutines call `c.reconcile(key)` → calls `c.reconciler(namespace, name, obj)`

This is used by the ValidatingAdmissionPolicy/MutatingAdmissionPolicy controllers in the apiserver, not by kube-controller-manager controllers.

The task is complete. `/workspace/answer.json` has been written with a full trace of how controllers register with the "controller-runtime Manager" in `kubernetes/kubernetes` v1.32.0.

## Key findings documented in answer.json

### 1. `sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime` is absent
The library is not vendored. `vendor/sigs.k8s.io/` contains only `json`, `yaml`, `kustomize`, `structured-merge-diff`, `knftables`, and `apiserver-network-proxy`. The only occurrence of the string `"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime"` in the entire repo is a comment in a test file.

### 2. Internal framework — the true analogues

| controller-runtime concept | kubernetes/kubernetes equivalent | File |
|---|---|---|
| `reconcile.Reconciler` interface | `controller.Interface` | `staging/src/k8s.io/controller-manager/controller/interfaces.go` |
| `SetupWithManager(mgr)` | `InitFunc` type + `newXxxControllerDescriptor()` | `cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go` |
| `ctrl.Manager` | `ControllerContext` struct | `cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go` |
| `mgr.Add(controller)` | `register()` inside `NewControllerDescriptors()` | `cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go` |
| `mgr.Start(ctx)` | `Run()` → `StartControllers()` → `StartController()` | `cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go` |

### 3. Registration call chain
```
Run(ctx, c)
  └─ run(ctx, controllerDescriptors)
       ├─ CreateControllerContext()   → builds ControllerContext (the "Manager")
       ├─ StartControllers()
       │    └─ StartController()      → calls initFunc → go controller.Run(ctx)
       └─ InformerFactory.Start()     → starts all shared informers
```

### 4. Generic controller-runtime-like pattern
`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/policy/internal/generic/` contains a Go-generics-based `Controller[T]` interface with a `reconciler` callback — the closest thing to controller-runtime's `Reconciler` pattern in the repo, used by admission policy controllers.
